message:
fix(mvcc/wal): add seqlock, fix concurrency bugs, and harden arithmetic safety

Add a new SeqLock<T> module for optimistic sub-nanosecond metadata reads
(schema, pragma settings) using even/odd sequence counter protocol. Writers
are serialized externally; readers never block writers.

MVCC invariants fixes:
- Fix race condition in alloc_commit_seq: acquire active_commits lock
  before fetching the sequence number to prevent stable_commit_seq from
  jumping past an in-flight commit.
- Use fetch_max instead of store for stable_commit_seq to handle
  concurrent finish_commit calls safely.
- Replace let-chain syntax with nested if-let for broader Rust edition
  compatibility in VersionStore::install_version.

SSI abort policy:
- Guard against empty residuals vec in ConformalCalibrator::upper_bound()
  to prevent subtract-with-overflow panic on the quantile index.

Two-phase commit:
- Use checked arithmetic (checked_mul/checked_add) when computing the
  needed buffer size for GlobalCommitMarker deserialization to prevent
  integer overflow on malformed input.

WAL fixes:
- Silence unused-variable warning in group_commit test.
- Fix potential truncation in frame count calculation by performing
  division in u64 before converting to usize.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
